source_paper_title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models Are Optimally Adaptive to Unknown Low Dimensionality
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Huang_2026, title={Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models Are Optimally Adaptive to Unknown Low Dimensionality}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2024.0769}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2024.0769}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Huang, Zhihan and Wei, Yuting and Chen, Yuxin}, year={2026}, month=mar }
context:
Section 6 (Discussion), page 16.

To begin with, it remains unclear how the DDPM sampler adapts to unknown low dimensionality when other metrics (e.g., the total-variation distance or the Wasserstein distance) are used to measure distributional discrepancy.
title: Characterize DDPM low-dimensional adaptivity under Wasserstein or total variation metrics
background_latex:
Let \(p_{\mathrm{data}}\) be a probability distribution on \(\mathbb{R}^d\) (the target data distribution). Consider the Ornstein--Uhlenbeck forward diffusion \((X_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) defined by
\[dX_t=-X_t\,dt+\sqrt{2}\,dW_t,\qquad X_0\sim p_{\mathrm{data}},\]
where \((W_t)\) is standard Brownian motion in \(\mathbb{R}^d\). Let \(q_t\) denote the law of \(X_t\).

The (Stein) score function at time \(t\) is \(s_t(x)=\nabla \log q_t(x)\) (for those \(t\) for which \(q_t\) admits a density). The denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM) sampler produces a discrete-time approximation to the reverse-time generative dynamics using a time grid \(0=t_0<t_1<\cdots<t_N<T\). Starting from \(Y_{t_0}\sim \mathcal{N}(0,I_d)\), DDPM iterates
\[Y_{t_{n+1}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\alpha_n}}\Big(Y_{t_n}+(1-\alpha_n)\,\widehat s_{T-t_n}(Y_{t_n})\Big)+\sqrt{\frac{(1-\alpha_n)(1-\bar\alpha_{n+1})}{1-\bar\alpha_n}}\,Z_n,\qquad Z_n\sim\mathcal{N}(0,I_d),\]
where \(\alpha_n=e^{-2(t_{n+1}-t_n)}\) and \(\bar\alpha_n=e^{-2(T-t_n)}\), and where \(\widehat s_t\) is an estimator of \(s_t\) (in the idealized case \(\widehat s_t=s_t\)). Let \(p_{\mathrm{out}}\) denote the law of the output \(Y_{t_N}\).

The paper studies intrinsic low dimensionality through a metric-entropy notion: the support \(\mathcal{X}_{\mathrm{data}}\subseteq\mathbb{R}^d\) of \(p_{\mathrm{data}}\) has intrinsic dimension \(k\) if its Euclidean covering numbers satisfy an upper bound of the form \(\log N_{\mathrm{cover}}(\mathcal{X}_{\mathrm{data}},\|\cdot\|_2,\varepsilon_0)\lesssim k\log(1/\varepsilon_0)\) at a small scale \(\varepsilon_0\) (as specified in the paper).

The paper’s main convergence guarantees quantify the sampling error in Kullback--Leibler divergence \(\mathrm{KL}(q_\delta\|p_{\mathrm{out}})\) for an early-stopping time \(\delta>0\) (so \(q_\delta\) is the law of the noisy data \(X_\delta\)). The open question asks for analogous adaptivity guarantees when the sampling error is measured in other probability metrics, such as total variation distance \(\|\mu-\nu\|_{\mathrm{TV}}\) or Wasserstein distance \(W_p(\mu,\nu)\) (for some \(p\ge 1\)).
problem_definition_latex:
Under the same type of low-dimensional structural conditions on \(p_{\mathrm{data}}\) (e.g., intrinsic dimension \(k\) defined via metric entropy of its support) and comparable regularity/estimation conditions on the score \(\widehat s_t\), establish non-asymptotic bounds on the number of DDPM steps \(N\) (as a function of \(k\) and an accuracy parameter \(\varepsilon\)) sufficient to guarantee small sampling error measured in a metric other than KL divergence, for example:

1) Total variation error: \(\|q_\delta-p_{\mathrm{out}}\|_{\mathrm{TV}}\le \varepsilon\).

2) Wasserstein error: \(W_p(q_\delta,p_{\mathrm{out}})\le \varepsilon\) for some \(p\ge 1\).

In particular, determine whether DDPM exhibits adaptivity to intrinsic dimension \(k\) under these metrics, analogous to the nearly linear-in-\(k\) iteration complexity established in KL divergence in the paper.
latex:
Let \(p_{\mathrm{data}}\) be a probability distribution on \(\mathbb{R}^d\) (the target data distribution). Consider the Ornstein--Uhlenbeck forward diffusion \((X_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) defined by

Under the same type of low-dimensional structural conditions on \(p_{\mathrm{data}}\) (e.g., intrinsic dimension \(k\) defined via metric entropy of its support) and comparable regularity/estimation conditions on the score \(\widehat s_t\), establish non-asymptotic bounds on the number of DDPM steps \(N\) (as a function of \(k\) and an accuracy parameter \(\varepsilon\)) sufficient to guarantee small sampling error measured in a metric other than KL divergence, for example:

In particular, determine whether DDPM exhibits adaptivity to intrinsic dimension \(k\) under these metrics, analogous to the nearly linear-in-\(k\) iteration complexity established in KL divergence in the paper.
open_problem_quote: To begin with, it remains unclear how the DDPM sampler adapts to unknown low dimensionality when other metrics (e.g., the total-variation distance or the Wasserstein distance) are used to measure distributional discrepancy.
subject_classification: theory of diffusion-model sampling and non-asymptotic convergence metrics
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question is qualitative and metric-dependent rather than a crisp inequality with a finite-dimensional parameter search; constructing definitive counterexamples would require designing distributions and analyzing sampler dynamics under TV/Wasserstein, which is not readily automatable at scale.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: There is no clear automatically checkable fitness function for candidate proofs or bounds: evaluating whether a proposed rate holds would require substantial symbolic reasoning and problem-specific analysis rather than running code on instances.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
